results.title: Certain Health-Ade Kombucha Flavors Contain Toxic ‘Forever Chemicals,’ Class Action Claims [UPDATE]
results.text:
Certain Health-Ade Kombucha Flavors Contain Toxic ‘Forever Chemicals,’ Class Action Claims
*in*[Newswire](https://classaction.org/news)Published on January 11, 2024
# Certain Health-Ade Kombucha Flavors Contain Toxic ‘Forever Chemicals,’ Class Action Claims[UPDATE]
by[Kelsey McCroskey](https://classaction.org/about-us/kelsey-mccroskey)
Last Updated on September 19, 2024
**Share[View Comments](#comments)
### Morton v. Health-Ade LLC
Filed: January 9, 2024◆[§ 7:24-cv-00173](#embedded-document)
[•Read Complaint](#embedded-document)
A class action alleges Health-Ade has misled consumers by marketing certain flavors of its kombucha as “health” products, when the drinks, in fact, contain dangerous chemicals.
Defendant(s)
[Health-Ade LLC](https://classaction.org/news/category/health-ade-llc)
Law(s)
[New York General Business Law](https://classaction.org/news/category/new-york-general-business-law)
State(s)
New York
Categories
[Food](https://classaction.org/news/category/food)[False Advertising](https://classaction.org/news/category/false-advertising)[Fraud](https://classaction.org/news/category/fraud)[PFAS](https://classaction.org/news/category/pfas)
## September 19, 2024 –Health-Ade Kombucha PFAS Lawsuit Settled With Plaintiff
The proposed class action lawsuit detailed on this page was discontinued early this month after the parties reached a private settlement.
After the filing of an amended complaint and a subsequent attempt by Health-Ade to dismiss the suit, the parties jointly notified the court in a one-page[letter](https://www.classaction.org/media/morton-v-health-ade-llc-letter.pdf)on September 5, 2024 that they had resolved the case and would be filing a request for[dismissal](https://www.classaction.org/blog/why-do-class-action-lawsuits-get-dismissed)soon.
In a one-page[order](https://www.classaction.org/media/morton-v-health-ade-llc-order.pdf)filed the next day, United States District Judge Cathy Seibel discontinued the[class action suit](https://www.classaction.org/learn/what-is-a-class-action)with prejudice, noting that the parties have 30 days to consummate the settlement before the plaintiff will be allowed to restore the case.

Health-Ade LLC deceptively markets its line of kombucha drinks as healthful despite a high sugar content that contradicts their name, according to a new would-be class action in a California federal court.

The brand “Health-Ade” conveys the message that the fermented tea drinks “aid health,” and the company’s marketing has helped its products become the fastest-selling kombucha in America, Californians Brandon Johnson-Jack and Michael Xavier allege.

Beverages sweetened with high amounts of added sugar, like the Health-Ade fermented teas, increase the risk of heart disease, liver disease, diabetes, and other illnesses, according to the suit, filed Thursday in the U.S. ...

results.title: Health-Ade Kombucha Increases Risk of Heart Disease, Diabetes, Class Action Alleges
results.author: Anna Bradley-Smith
results.text:
window.googletag = window.googletag || { cmd: [] }; googletag.cmd.push(function () { googletag.pubads().set("page_url", "https://topclassactions.com/"); }); -- Health-Ade Kombucha Increases Risk of Heart Disease, Diabetes, Class Action Alleges -- -- -- --

# Health-Ade Kombucha Increases Risk of Heart Disease, Diabetes, Class Action Alleges

(Photo Credit: Sheila Fitzgerald/Shutterstock)

## Health-Ade Kombucha Class Action Lawsuit Overview:

- Where: The lawsuit was filed in California on behalf of consumers nationwide
- Why: Consumers say the drinks, which are marketed as being healthy, actually increase the risk of numerous metabolic diseases
- Who: Health-Ade has been hit with a class action lawsuit alleging false advertising of its kombucha drinks
- Member Center

---

Despite being marketed as a product that increases gut and all round health, the high levels of sugar in Health-Ade Kombucha increases risks of metabolic disease, cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, liver disease, and all-cause mortality, a new class action lawsuit alleges.

The class action was filed in California on October 7 by lead plaintiffs Brandon Johnson-Jack and Michael Xavier, who allege Health-Ade LLC has falsely advertised and sold its line of kombucha or kombucha-inspired beverages violating state and federal laws.

Because the Health-Ade beverages contain a high amount of added sugar, the company’s representations the drinks will aid health are false and misleading, the pair say, since consuming beverages sweetened with high amounts of added sugar increase the risk of a number of diseases.

Johnson-Jack and Xavier bought around 16 bottles of the kombucha drinks (which are made from tea, water, sugar, and a culture of yeast and bacteria for fermentation) each, both say they were under the impression the drinks were beneficial to their health.

“Defendant prominently labeled and continues to label the Health-Ade Beverages with the claim, ‘Health-Ade,’ which expressly conveys the message that the Health-Ade Beverages are healthy,” the pair alleges in the lawsuit.

“This claim, however, was and is deceptive because the products do not aid health, but instead, their high sugar content makes their regular consumption likely to increase the risk of disease.”

## Health-Ade Kombucha Comes With Not-So-Healthy Dose of Sugar

Each standard serving of the kombucha contains between 12 to 17 grams of sugar — including 10 to 13 grams of added sugar — accounting for between 60 to 91 percent of the products’ calories, the claim reads.

Johnson-Jack and Xavier say in the lawsuit that excess added sugar consumption leads to metabolic syndrome by stressing and damaging crucial organs, including the pancreas and liver.

“Metabolic disease has been linked to type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, obesity, polycystic ovary syndrome, nonalcoholic fatty liver disease, and chronic kidney disease,” they say.

Even if the pro- or prebiotics in the Health-Ade beverages were capable of

results.title: Class Action Alleges ‘Health-Ade’ Kombucha Products Mislabeled Since They Contain Added Sugar
results.text:
Class Action Alleges ‘Health-Ade’ Kombucha Products Mislabeled Since They Contain Added Sugar

Share View Comments

### Johnson-Jack et al. v. Health-Ade LLC

Filed: October 7, 2021 ◆§ 3:21-cv-07895

• Read Complaint

A class action claims Health-Ade kombucha and kombucha-inspired beverages are not as healthy as represented given they contain a “high amount” of added sugar.

Defendant(s)

Health-Ade LLC

Law(s)

California Business and Professions Code

State(s)

California

Categories

Food False Advertising Fraud

New to ClassAction.org? Read our Newswire Disclaimer

A proposed class action claims Health-Ade kombucha and kombucha-inspired beverages are not as healthy as represented given they contain a “high amount” of added sugar.

The 37-page lawsuit alleges defendant Health-Ade LLC has falsely and misleadingly represented that certain products, including Health-Ade Kombucha, Health-Ade Plus, Health-Ade Booch Pop, Health-Ade pop and Health-Ade Mixers, will benefit consumers’ health while failing to disclose that the beverages’ high sugar content could contribute to the risk of metabolic disease, cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, liver disease and even all-cause mortality.

According to the lawsuit, the defendant is “well aware” that consumers prefer and are willing to pay more for foods marketed as healthy. Labeling and branding its products as “Health-Ade,” which the suit says expressly conveys that the drinks will aid consumers’ health, has helped the company’s line of kombucha products become the fastest-selling in the U.S., the case claims.

The lawsuit alleges, however, that the defendant’s kombucha—a drink produced by fermenting tea with a yeast and bacteria culture—and kombucha-inspired products are not as healthy as consumers are led to believe given their high sugar content. Per the complaint, the sugar in Health-Ade drinks comprises anywhere from 50 to 91 percent of their total calories.

“Instead of receiving products that had actual healthful qualities, the Health-Ade Beverages that Plaintiffs and the Class received were beverages of the type that are likely to lead to increased risk of disease when consumed regularly,” the complaint says, claiming that the drinks were worth less than consumers paid for them.

The filing cites a plethora of scientific studies and evidence demonstrating that the consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as the Health-Ade products is unhealthy and linked to a risk of metabolic disease, cardiovascular heart disease, type 2 diabetes, liver disease, obesity, high blood triglycerides, abnormal cholesterol levels, hypertension, poor gut health and all-cause mortality.

Given the health risks associated with consuming sugar-sweetened beverages, the defendant’s representations of its Health-Ade products as healthy are “false, or at least highly misleading,” the lawsuit alleges. Moreover, even if the pro- or pre-biotics in the Health-Ade products can provide some benefit to consumers’ gut health,

Health-Ade LLC deceptively markets kombucha drinks as “health” products despite the presence of dangerous levels of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances, a new proposed class action alleges.

The suit adds to a growing list of lawsuits over PFAS in various consumer products.

Testing by an independent laboratory analysis revealed PFAS chemicals in Kombucha Ginger Pineapple Belly Reset and four other products, Alanna Morton says in a suit filed in the US District Court for the Southern District of New York.

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has outlined a host of health effects associated with PFAS exposure, including cancer, liver ...

results.title: Health-Ade Kombucha Review - Must Read This Before Buying
results.text:
Health-Ade Kombucha Review - Must Read This Before Buying 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Wellness

# Health-Ade Kombucha Review

Table of Contents

## About Health-Ade

If you’re interested in wellness and nutrition, then you’re bound to have heard of kombucha. This super healthy drink has become a craze in the last few years, with consumers loving its delicious taste and nutritional benefits. However, as studies note, not all kombucha is the same.

Health-Ade is passionate about making the best-tasting, highest-quality kombucha using only natural ingredients. And, despite there being quite a few kombucha brands on the market now, this one seems to stand out from the crowd.

Health-Ade was founded in 2012 in Los Angeles, CA by a husband and wife team, Justin and Daina Trout, and their best friend, Vanessa Drew. The kombucha was first available at LA farmer’s markets, but the brand quickly expanded to selling in stores and via its website.

The founders had a dream to make incredibly tasting, healthy, and super high-quality kombucha. With customers’ wellness in mind, the brand focuses on being a “champion of the happiest and healthiest you.”

Today, Health-Ade boasts 29 yummy flavors of kombucha across four different collections. While some products are meant to be enjoyed right when you open the bottle or can, the company also offers mixers, which can add a dose of nutrition to your favorite alcoholic or non-alcoholic drink.

Along with tasting amazing, all Health-Ade kombucha is certified vegan and uses non-GMO and USDA organic ingredients. The company also supports ethical conditions for all workers involved in the production and distribution of its products.

 

Additionally, the brand ensures it reaches out to the community. Recently, it has teamed up with the Garden School Foundation to help underprivileged children learn about gardening and nutrition.

So, is this the brand you should turn to for your next bottle of nutritious goodness? This Health-Ade kombucha review will help you figure that out. I’ll look at the company, its bestsellers, customer ratings, and more, so stay tuned.

shop now

## Pros and Cons

### Pros

1. High-Quality Ingredients: Health-Ade prides itself on using high-quality, organic ingredients in their kombucha. They use real fruits, herbs, and teas, and avoid artificial flavors or preservatives.
2. Fermented and Probiotic-Rich: Kombucha is a fermented beverage that contains live cultures and probiotics, which are beneficial for gut health. Health-Ade kombucha undergoes a natural fermentation process, resulting in a probiotic-rich product.
3. Flavor Variety: Health-Ade offers a wide range of flavors, catering to different taste preferences. From classic flavors like Ginger-Lemon to unique combinations like Pink Lady Apple and Bubbly Rose, there is a flavor for everyone.
4. Transparency and Sustainability: Health-Ade emphasizes transparency in their manufacturing processes. They provide detailed information about their ingr

## Sustainability features

As certified by

## USDA Organic

---

USDA Organic certified products are required to be produced using farming practices that maintain and improve soil and water quality, reduce the use of synthetic materials, conserve biodiversity, and avoid genetic engineering, among other factors. Crops can be certified organic if they're grown without prohibited substances such as most synthetic fertilizers and pesticides for three years prior to harvest. Livestock are raised on pastures and treated humanely without growth hormones or antibiotics. Organic products do not contain GMOs or artificial colours, flavours or preservatives. USDA Organic products contain a minimum of 95% organic ingredients and use the USDA Organic seal.
